game_name: Advanced Dungeons & Dragons: Pools of Radiance

element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Six races and four classes (Fighter, Cleric, Magic-User, Thief) establish foundational mechanics; multi-classing available for non-humans with level progression trade-off
Three-dimensional first-person perspective with split-screen UI showing party status and area information; players learn spatial navigation
Encounters follow AD&D encounter tables; three sprite variations indicate encounter range (short/medium/long) to teach distance assessment
Switches to top-down dimetric projection; immediate action resolution; class-specific combat options (melee/ranged for fighters, spellcasting for magic-users, backstab for thieves)
Up to 6 characters with coordinated actions; characters can bandage wounded allies below 0 HP to prevent death; positional strategy matters (flanking, retreat mechanics)
Menu-accessed spell system; learned gradually as characters level; enables new tactical approaches
Buy/sell items; trade between party members; pool money; reflects traditional D&D resource management
Parley with opponents; NPCs can fill two open party slots; alignment affects NPC reactions to player actions
Experience points increase capabilities; leveling unlocked through AD&D mechanics; fighter multi-attacks and sweep abilities unlock at higher levels
Ultimate confrontation with powerful leader of evil forces; synthesizes all prior mechanics

typical_relief_triggers: Town/hub area access for buying/selling and resting
Successful combat encounter completion and experience gain
NPC recruitment and party slot expansion
typical_tension_triggers: Character HP drop below critical threshold
Unknown enemy composition or monster types encountered
Isometric combat perspective switch (signal of danger)
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Cyclical exploration-combat-progression loop with gradual difficulty scaling; random encounters inject unpredictability; towns provide respite and planning between exploration segments

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Graveyard area explicitly mentioned as explorable space; suggests optional locations exist within port city of Phlan and surrounding areas

difficulty_curve.early_game: Tutorial-like character creation and first random encounters establish mechanics; pre-generated party option available for new players
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Progressive monster difficulty following AD&D encounter tables; character leveling accelerates party power
difficulty_curve.late_game: Culminates in powerful leader confrontation requiring full party synthesis of all learned mechanics
notable_spikes: First combat encounter (transition from safe exploration to real stakes)
Final boss encounter with evil forces leader

notable_level_patterns.description: Port city of Phlan serves as safe settlement hub for character management, item trading, and NPC recruitment; surrounded by marauding-inhabited wilderness areas requiring systematic clearing

Random encounters follow standard AD&D encounter table patterns, creating predictable but varied threat sequences; sprite range variations (short/medium/long) communicate spatial difficulty positioning
Fighter multi-attacks and sweep abilities unlock at specific levels; spell learning progression for magic-users/clerics; creates level-gated content within same areas
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Any combat encounter, replayed with higher-level party
